changes.rationale: The infimum is the greatest lower bound. Its statement requires only a lower bound and the order relation. The least-upper-bound PROPERTY is a completeness consequence used to PROVE that infima exist, not a concept needed to DEFINE one. This is a deductive (existence) dependency miscategorized as a definitional one - exactly the error a definition's closure touching a completeness-type node should surface.
changes.licensing_quote: i is a lower bound of A and every lower bound l of A satisfies l <= i

notes: Do NOT route this to ax:completeness-of-reals. Completeness belongs to the existence THEOREM (thm:lub-property-r / a dual greatest-lower-bound existence theorem), never to the definition.
